name: summary-box
title: Summary box
description: Bordered, emphasized box that highlights key information or next steps (e.g. "What you'll need") so users do not have to hunt for it. A labelled region named by its heading, with a subtle tint and border; structural rather than status-colored, so it stays legible in forced-colors mode.
useWhen: Summarizing the documents or information a user needs before starting a task
Listing the next steps in a process so they are easy to find
Pulling key takeaways out of longer content into an emphasized box
avoidWhen: Reporting a status, result, or warning (use Alert)
A sitewide emergency or service notice at the top of every page (use Site alert)
Long-form content that is better served by ordinary headings and prose

keyboard: The box is static content and never takes focus
It is a region landmark labelled by its heading (aria-labelledby), so screen-reader users can navigate to it and hear its name
The heading participates in the page outline at the level set by headingLevel
Any links inside follow the native anchor keyboard contract and are always underlined
